reasoning: User asks for cheap accommodation in Kyoto under 5,000 yen/night. The tool returns a general hotel list with no price data whatsoever — no price_band, no rates. Hotels include DoubleTree by Hilton which is well above budget. The budget constraint (¥5,000/night) is completely unaddressed. The response is grounded in real hotels but useless for the specific ask.
missing_data: Price/rate information per hotel; hostel/guesthouse/capsule options; price_band field for budget filtering
improvement_hint: Add price_band field to hotel records; support filtering by lodging_type (hostel, capsule, guesthouse) to surface budget options; surface hostels first when query lang is non-English and budget intent detectable.

reasoning: User wants free activities in Tokyo. Response returns general top spots including Hamarikyu (fee=yes, price_band=low) and National Museum of Western Art (fee=yes). Mount Takao (price_band=free) is present. Free constraint only partially respected. Useful spots present but free-entry filtering is not applied, so agent must sift through paid spots. Missing notable free destinations like Shinjuku Gyoen free grounds, Yanaka, Ueno Park.
missing_data: Explicit free=yes filtered list; Ueno Park, Yanaka, Shinjuku Gyoen free sections, Tokyo Metropolitan Government Observatory
improvement_hint: Support fee=no filter parameter in get_spots; add fee field prominently to allow agent-side filtering; ensure free outdoor spots (parks, shrine grounds) are well-covered.

reasoning: User asks for free-entry temples/shrines in Kamakura. Hybrid search on Japanese terms returns mostly irrelevant results: a Kanagawa tourism planning page, a Fukui cat temple, Eiheiji (700 yen fee), and Joraku-ji (600 yen fee). None of these are free Kamakura temples. The response fails to surface Kamakura's well-known free shrines (Tsurugaoka Hachimangu, Sasuke Inari, etc.). Constraint completely unmet.
missing_data: Kamakura-specific free shrines: Tsurugaoka Hachimangu, Sasuke Inari Shrine, Zeniarai Benten, Goryo Jinja; fee=no field filtering
improvement_hint: Add prefecture scoping to hybrid search; improve BM25 query to include municipality name; add fee metadata to shrine/temple records so free-entry spots can be surfaced directly.

reasoning: User wants Fukuoka yatai (street food stalls) under 500 yen. Response returns MAFF GI products (Yame tea, hakata chicken) and a scraped item about cherry blossoms — none are yatai stalls or price-relevant. The ¥500 budget constraint and yatai-specific request are completely unaddressed. get_local_food is the wrong tool for this; there's no yatai data at all. Total constraint failure.
missing_data: Yatai stall information, Fukuoka Nakasu/Tenjin yatai locations, price range data for street food
wrong_data: Cherry Blossoms scraped item is mislabeled as regional_dish
improvement_hint: Add yatai/street food category to local food data; fix scraper to reject tourism portal pages misclassified as regional dishes; add price_range field to food items.

reasoning: User asks for free-entry shrines/temples in Kyoto in Chinese. search_area returns 100 Kyoto shrines but has no price/fee data — cannot distinguish free from paid. Results are obscure rural shrines with no descriptions, not the famous free Kyoto shrines (Fushimi Inari, Heian Jingu, Kamigamo). The free-entry constraint is ignored entirely. Multilingual names are sparse.
missing_data: Fee/price_band data on shrines; prominent free Kyoto shrines (Fushimi Inari, Heian Jingu); Chinese name translations for most results
improvement_hint: Add fee field to shrine/temple records in scraper; ensure Kyoto's major free-entry religious sites have explicit fee=no tagging; improve Chinese name coverage for Kyoto shrines.

reasoning: User asks for cheap eats in Sapporo markets/restaurants under ¥1,500. Response returns 500+ MAFF GI products and ingredients (Yubari Melon, Tokachi yam, potatoes) — these are agricultural commodities, not restaurants or markets. No market venue data, no restaurant data, no price data. Sapporo-specific market info (Nijo Market, Curb Market) entirely absent. Complete constraint failure on budget and venue type.
missing_data: Sapporo Nijo Market, Sapporo Curb Market, affordable ramen/soup curry spots, market vendor information
improvement_hint: Add market venue category to spots data; distinguish between food products (ingredients) and food experiences (markets, restaurants) in get_local_food output.

reasoning: User asks for cheap hostels in Osaka under ¥3,000/night. Tool returns hotel list without price data. Results include ANA Crowne Plaza (luxury), DoubleTree by Hilton (upscale) — clearly above budget. Some potential cheap options present (Alex's room, Diamond Hotel) but no price_band/rate data to confirm. Hostel-type filter not applied. Budget constraint ¥3,000 completely unaddressed.
missing_data: Price per night / price_band data; hostel-specific filtering; Osaka budget hostel districts (Shinsaibashi, Namba area)
improvement_hint: Add price_band field to hotel records; support lodging_type=hostel filter in get_hotels; surface osm-sourced guesthouses and hostels with budget metadata.

reasoning: User asks about cheap souvenirs at 100-yen shops and supermarkets. Tool called is get_local_specialty for Aichi food, which returns only 3 MAFF GI products (Hatcho miso, winter melon, perilla flower spikes). This completely misses the intent — 100-yen shops and supermarket souvenir hunting is about retail store recommendations, not GI-designated ingredients. The see_also venues are art museums. No relevance to the actual question.
missing_data: 100-yen shop locations in Aichi; supermarkets near tourist areas; affordable omiyage shops in Nagoya; Nagoya meibutsu items available at convenience stores
improvement_hint: Add retail shop category for omiyage/souvenir stores to spots data; distinguish GI ingredients from affordable packaged souvenirs; consider a dedicated souvenir shop tool or category.

reasoning: User asks for free outdoor parks/nature across Japan. Hybrid search returns mixed results: one relevant result (Nishiyama Park with free admission, free zoo), Mount Takao (implicitly free hiking), but also irrelevant municipal website pages (Hikari City tourism, Miyoshi city nature page). Top result is a tourism portal website, not a named spot. Results not filtered for fee=free. Useful data exists but buried.
missing_data: Explicitly free parks with fee=no field; national parks (free to enter), urban parks like Ueno, Yoyogi; explicit free-entry confirmation
improvement_hint: Filter hybrid search results to return only spots (not tourism portal pages); add fee=no field; prioritize well-known free national parks and city parks.

reasoning: Korean query for free parks in Tokyo. Response uses same get_spots(Tokyo) as R-277. Mount Takao (price_band=free) is a good result. However Hamarikyu has fee=yes. National Museum of Western Art has fee=yes. The free-entry constraint is not filtered. Korean name fields (name_ko) are populated which is good for Korean users. Response provides useful spots but the free filter is not applied, requiring agent to manually identify free spots.
missing_data: Explicit free parks: Shinjuku Gyoen (partly free), Yoyogi Park, Ueno Park, Inokashira Park; fee=no filtering
improvement_hint: Add fee filter option to get_spots; ensure major free Tokyo parks have explicit fee=no; prioritize outdoor/park kinds when free constraint detected.

reasoning: Arabic query for cheap/free Nagano attractions. Response returns 30 spots including Kamikochi (national park, free access), Matsushiro Castle (fee=no, price_band=free), Takato Castle (price_band=low), and others. Some free spots are surfaced with explicit fee=no. However no Arabic translations available (name_ar fields missing), limiting utility for Arabic-speaking agents. Coverage is decent but free-entry filtering still relies on agent post-processing.
missing_data: Arabic translations for spot names; more spots with explicit free-entry confirmation; onsen/hot spring free foot baths
improvement_hint: Add Arabic (ar) to translation pipeline for spot names; add fee filter to get_spots; ensure mountain parks and shrine grounds with free access are tagged fee=no.

reasoning: User seeks luxury honeymoon onsen ryokan in Hokkaido. Response includes Nihon Hito-yu secret hot spring ryokans (authentic onsen) plus ANA Holiday Inn and other hotels. The hitoyu-kai ryokans are good matches (authentic onsen, remote, romantic) but lack English names, coordinates, websites, and phone numbers — critical for luxury booking. No tier/luxury indicator. Budget 'no limit' means high-end filtering needed but absent.
missing_data: English names for ryokans; websites/booking URLs; coordinates for hitoyu_kai entries; luxury tier indicator; specific room type info (private open-air bath)
improvement_hint: Enrich hito_yu_kai records with coordinates, websites, English names from the official website; add luxury_tier field; surface hito_yu_kai entries before generic hotels for onsen ryokan queries.

reasoning: User wants luxury ryokan in Kyoto for honeymoon with kaiseki and private open-air bath. Same hotel list as R-276 (Kyoto hotels). Results are dominated by business hotels (AB Hotel, APA, Comfort Inn) — exactly opposite of luxury. No ryokan type appears. No kaiseki or private bath data. The luxury/ryokan constraint is completely ignored. This is identical data to the budget query case, showing no differentiation.
missing_data: Kyoto luxury ryokans (Tawaraya, Hiiragiya, Gion Hatanaka); onsen_ryokan type filtering; amenity data (kaiseki, private bath)
improvement_hint: Add lodging_type=ryokan filter to get_hotels; enrich ryokan records with amenity tags (kaiseki, private_bath, open_air_bath); source Kyoto premium ryokan data from official ryokan associations.

reasoning: Chinese query for luxury ocean-view resort in Okinawa for honeymoon. Response starts with unnamed hostels and guesthouses (lowest budget options), then DoubleTree and Hilton Chatan Resort (good matches). No Chinese names for most properties. No sea-view or resort-type filter. The luxury beach resort constraint is partially addressable from the data but hostels and unnamed properties crowd the top of the list. Ordering does not match user intent at all.
missing_data: Chinese name translations for Hilton/DoubleTree; explicit beach/ocean-view attribute; luxury tier indicator; well-known Okinawa resorts (Busena Terrace, Manza Beach Hotel)
improvement_hint: Rank onsen_ryokan and named brand hotels before unnamed OSM entries; add resort/beach attribute; expand Chinese name coverage for Okinawa hotels.

reasoning: French query for luxury honeymoon in Kyoto+Nara. Tool only queries Nara (half the request ignored). Response includes JW Marriott Nara (good luxury match) and hitoyu-kai onsen ryokan (Yado Yu-no-Sato — remote mountain onsen, romantic). Also includes Super Hotel Lohas and a motel (inappropriate for luxury). No French name translations. Kyoto completely missing. Mixed luxury/budget results without filtering.
missing_data: Kyoto luxury hotels/ryokans (query only covers Nara); French name translations; amenity data for romantic/luxury filtering
improvement_hint: When query spans two prefectures, call tool for both; add lodging_type filter; ensure motel results are filtered out for luxury queries; add French translations to major hotel names.

reasoning: User wants free/cheap sake tasting at Niigata breweries. Response returns famous sake brands (Koshi no Kanbai, Hakkaisan, Kubota) — good brand identification — plus GI-listed edamame (not sake). However, no brewery venue data, visiting hours, tasting fees, or addresses. Sake brands identified but the tasting experience (location, cost, booking) is entirely missing. MAFF GI items like Kurosaki Chamame are irrelevant to the sake query.
missing_data: Brewery visitor center info; tasting fee/free tasting data; brewery addresses and hours; Ponshukan sake museum in Niigata City (free tasting with tokens)
improvement_hint: Add sake brewery venue type to spots data; link wikidata sake brand records to physical brewery locations; add tasting_available and tasting_fee fields.

reasoning: Korean query for budget restaurants/markets in Sapporo under ¥1,000/meal. Identical structure to R-281 (same Hokkaido get_local_food). Returns MAFF GI agricultural products — Yubari Melon, Tokachi yam, potato. Korean translations are good quality. However no restaurant, market venue, or price data. The ¥1,000 budget and market/restaurant venue constraint completely unmet. Nijo Market, ramen shops absent.
missing_data: Sapporo Nijo Market venue; affordable ramen shops; soup curry restaurants; price_range data for dining experiences
improvement_hint: Separate food product data (ingredients) from food experience data (venues); add market/restaurant category; support price_range filtering.

reasoning: User asks about budget Yakushima with free hiking and cheap accommodation. get_spots for Yakushima municipality returns excellent data: the island itself (UNESCO WHS), Jomon Sugi (iconic hike, no entry fee implied), lighthouse (free outdoor), and crucially 屋久島青少年旅行村 (youth campsite at ¥440/night) — directly answering the cheap accommodation ask. Descriptions include access info, prices, and facilities. Strong match to intent. Minor gap: no explicit hiking trail fee info for Shiratani Unsuikyo (¥500 conservation fee).
missing_data: Shiratani Unsuikyo conservation fee info; Yakusugi Land entry fee; accommodation options beyond campsite
improvement_hint: Add price/fee field to municipal scrape spots; tag hiking trails with trail_fee where applicable; expand accommodation data for Yakushima beyond campsite.

reasoning: Vietnamese query for budget Kyoto travel under ¥5,000/night and free spots. Same hotel list as R-276 and R-288 (identical Kyoto hotel response). No Vietnamese name translations. No price data. No free spots included (wrong tool for spots). Budget and free-spots constraints both unaddressed. The response is structurally identical to other Kyoto hotel queries regardless of language or budget intent.
missing_data: Price_band/rate data; hostels and capsule hotels; free Kyoto spots (Fushimi Inari, Nijo Castle grounds, shrine grounds); Vietnamese translations
improvement_hint: Add price_band to hotel records; support multi-tool queries (hotels + spots) when user asks about both; add Vietnamese to name translation pipeline.

reasoning: User asks for free/cheap Tokyo museums including free national facilities. Hybrid search returns highly relevant results: International Museum Day free admission event (naming 5 free venues on that day), Tokyo National Museum, Edo-Tokyo Museum, and Min-On Music Museum (explicitly described as free, 無料). Tokyo Agricultural University Science Museum also appears. Good coverage of the intent with specific museum names, descriptions mentioning free admission. Results are mostly Tokyo-scoped and relevant.
missing_data: Permanent free museums: Science Museum in Kitanomaru Park; National Museum of Nature and Science free days; clear distinction of always-free vs. event-free
improvement_hint: Add permanent_free tag to always-free venues; separate event-based free days from standing free admission; add opening hours and admission fee fields to museum records.

reasoning: Spanish query for free attractions in Nara on tight budget. Response returns 30 spots including Takamatsuzuka Tomb (price_band=free), Kofuku-ji (price_band=free — though temple interior has fees), Mount Yoshino (free mountain access), Koriyama Castle. Some free spots visible. However no Spanish translations. Famous free Nara experiences (Nara Park with deer, free shrine grounds) not explicitly marked. Mix of free and paid without clear differentiation.
missing_data: Spanish name translations; Nara Park (deer park, free); Kasuga Taisha shrine grounds (free); explicit fee=no filtering
wrong_data: Kofuku-ji price_band=free is questionable — national treasure hall requires entry fee
improvement_hint: Add Spanish to translation pipeline; fix price_band for temples that have paid interior areas; add fee=no filter to get_spots; tag Nara Park/deer park as free.

reasoning: User wants exclusive luxury ryokan in Hakone with private open-air bath and kaiseki. Response starts with Aoki House hostel and Guest House Kamejikan — exact opposite of request. reasoning: German query for exclusive luxury onsen ryokan in Beppu for honeymoon. Response has hitoyu-kai onsen ryokans (山の宿 寒の地獄旅館, 山あいの宿 喜安屋) but both are in Kusu district, not Beppu city. Also has Onsenyado Hamayu Nagi (Beppu-area, onsen), AMANEK BEPPU YULA-RE (good match in Beppu). No German name translations. Hitoyu-kai entries lack coordinates and websites — critical for luxury booking. The luxury-exclusive constraint is partially met but booking info missing.
missing_data: German translations for ryokan names; coordinates/websites for hito_yu_kai entries; Beppu-specific luxury ryokans (Suginoi Hotel, Yukemuri no Yado Fufu); price/luxury tier

reasoning: Chinese query for low-cost local food culture in Kyushu, specifically restaurants/markets. Tool queries Kumamoto food only (one of 7 Kyushu prefectures). Returns MAFF GI products: igusa (rush grass for tatami — not edible!), tatami facing material (completely non-food), Kumamoto red beef, Kikuchi burdock. Rush grass for tatami is prominently listed as 'food' — data quality issue. No restaurant/market venues. Query scope (all Kyushu) not addressed. Budget constraint entirely missing.
missing_data: Kumamoto Shotengai (shopping street) markets; affordable restaurants; Fukuoka yatai coverage; correct food categorization (tatami material excluded)
wrong_data: くまもと県産い草 (rush grass for tatami) and い草畳表 (tatami facing) listed under food category — these are craft materials, not food
improvement_hint: Fix MAFF GI category mapping to exclude craft/textile products from food responses; add restaurant/market venue data; support multi-prefecture Kyushu-region query.

reasoning: User asks for free-admission cultural festivals worth attending across Japan. Tool queries only Aomori. Returns 2 high-quality results: Hachinohe Sansha Taisai and Aomori Nebuta Matsuri (both Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties). national_heritage section adds UNESCO-listed Dainichido Bugaku (Akita) and Hayachine Kagura (Iwate), Kawagoe Hikawa Festival (Saitama) — good cross-regional coverage. However no admission fee info (Nebuta has paid viewing stands but street viewing is free). National scope of query not addressed by Aomori-only call.
missing_data: Festival admission fee data; national-level coverage beyond Aomori; dates/months for attending festivals; viewing location advice (free street vs. paid stand)
wrong_data: Dainichido Bugaku description says 'Akita Prefecture' but it is categorized under Aomori — slight geographic mismatch in scope
improvement_hint: Add admission_fee field to festival records; support national/multi-prefecture query for festivals; add annual date/month field for trip planning; clarify street-view free vs. paid seating.
